mappings.rationaleAI: Covers archaeological methods essential for studying pre-Inca cultures (A), including Chavin, Paracas, Nazca, Moche, and Tiahuanaco sites (B.2-B.5).
Directly covers the Spanish conquest of the Incas (1532-40), Pizarro's execution of Atahuallpa, and consolidation of Spanish power (C.1.f).
Covers the Late Preceramic development of agriculture (B.1) and early Andean settlement patterns before the rise of complex societies.
Relevant to the ethnography of the Andean region (A), Incan government and society, and the quipu record-keeping system (C.2.a).
Covers documentary historical sources about the Incas and the Spanish conquest, including chronicles by Las Casas and Garcilaso de la Vega (A, C.1.f).
Provides context for understanding the Inca Empire as one of the great pre-modern empires, and the Spanish imperial conquest that destroyed it (C.1.f).
